tasks.description: Stabilize row materialization in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts` so grouped boundary commits no longer drift: switch `rowsToConfig()` to a shared 4-decimal policy, serialize hold clips from one rounded start/end pair, and update the focused render-bounds regression to prove repeated interior-boundary commits preserve adjacency with no gaps/overlaps.
tasks.executor_notes: rowsToConfig now applies a shared 4-decimal timing policy and derives hold durations from one rounded start/end pair so rematerialization keeps grouped interior boundaries stable. Added a repeated round-trip regression that keeps a 2.3334 boundary exact across six configToRows/rowsToConfig cycles with no gap or overlap. Verified with full-file runs of `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts` and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Add lost-pointer-capture teardown for unified resize sessions in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`: wire `onLostPointerCapture`, route it through the same `endResize()` cleanup path as pointer up/cancel, ensure interaction-end notification still fires, and extend `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` with a lost-capture regression that proves preview overrides clear and release behavior completes.
tasks.executor_notes: TimelineCanvas now routes `onLostPointerCapture` through the existing `endResize()` teardown, so the same cleanup path clears overrides, releases capture, decrements pending ops, resets `interactionStateRef.resize`, and notifies idle listeners. Added a lost-capture regression that proves preview width snaps back to committed rows, `onActionResizeEnd` fires with the previewed bounds, and the interaction-end listener runs. Verified with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and the combined targeted sweep.

tasks.description: Finish the type/migration layer rollback to soft-tag only: remove deprecated `PinnedShotGroup.start` and `PinnedShotGroup.children`, expand `PinnedShotImageClipSnapshot` only as needed to store absolute restore bounds, add one-way legacy-group repair in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts`, and tighten migration/serialization tests so projection-shaped input loads as `clipIds` only and legacy fields are never re-emitted.
tasks.executor_notes: Removed the deprecated `PinnedShotGroup.start` and `PinnedShotGroup.children` fields from the exported type, added optional snapshot `start`/`end` bounds for later image restore work, and kept legacy compatibility contained to `repairConfig()` via a local legacy cast. Migration now rewrites projection-shaped groups to ordered soft-tag `clipIds` only while preserving deep-cloned snapshot data, and serialization tests assert legacy fields are never re-emitted. Verified with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts` and the combined targeted sweep.

tasks.description: Rewrite pinned-group sync in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts` around live `clipIds` and row actions: remove compatibility-helper usage, diff `Shot.images[]` generation order against current clips, reuse/add/remove row actions directly, rebuild ordered `clipIds` with `orderClipIdsByAt()`, keep sync deferred during active interaction, and update the relevant pinned-group/shot-group tests to assert derived children/start come from live rows instead of legacy projection data.
tasks.executor_notes: `usePinnedShotGroups.ts` now pins and syncs groups as ordered soft-tag `clipIds` only, diffing `Shot.images[]` against live row actions, reusing/adding/removing row actions directly, and rescheduling sync while interaction is active. Updated `useShotGroups.test.ts`, `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, and the pinned-group sync assertions in `useClipEditing.test.ts` to assert live-row-derived membership instead of legacy projection fields. Verified with two full-file sweeps of `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`; the only remaining failures are the two untouched `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests in `useClipEditing.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Rewrite `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts` for the soft-tag model: snapshot each image clip's absolute state into `imageClipSnapshot`, switch to a direct final-video clip with `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, restore images from the snapshot through one soft-tag rows commit, and update `useClipEditing.test.ts` to cover both switch directions under the new data shape.
tasks.executor_notes: `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts` now snapshots image clips with absolute `start/end` bounds, switches groups to `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, and restores image rows from the snapshot without reading or writing legacy `start`/`children`. Updated the switch-to-video / switch-to-images coverage in `useClipEditing.test.ts` to assert soft-tag group state plus snapshot bounds. Verified inside the same two full-file sweeps; the updated T5 assertions pass, and the only remaining failures are the two untouched `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests elsewhere in `useClipEditing.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Rewrite external shot drop in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts` so row actions and group creation are committed directly in soft-tag form, preserving correct clip positions and ordered `clipIds`, and update `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` to validate the real row/meta/group result rather than compatibility-shim behavior.
tasks.executor_notes: `useExternalDrop.ts` now creates shot row actions and the pinned group in one `rows` commit using ordered soft-tag `clipIds`, with no projection-child construction. Updated `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` to assert the actual row/meta/group result. Verified with two full-file sweeps of the batch target suites; `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` passed cleanly in both runs.

tasks.description: Replace the remaining `moveGroup()`-style grouped track-move commits in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` with atomic soft-tag row/meta/order/group updates, ensuring cross-track moves also update group `trackId` and per-clip row assignment consistently with live preview, and update `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to cover the new commit shape.
tasks.executor_notes: `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` no longer uses `moveGroup()` at the remaining grouped move callsites. Grouped cross-track moves now translate live rows, patch each member’s `track` meta, update clip order, and rewrite the group’s `trackId` plus ordered `clipIds` atomically, including the create-new-track path. Updated `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to assert the new commit shapes. Verified with two full-file sweeps of the batch target suites; `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed cleanly in both runs.

tasks.description: Delete the deprecated compatibility layer and finish test realignment: remove `buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `replaceGroupChildren`, `moveGroup`, and `projectPinnedShotGroups` from `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts`; scrub remaining imports/usages; update `timeline-save-utils.test.ts` plus any remaining soft-tag test fixtures; remove diagnostic `console.log` calls for the listed tags; and verify cleanup with precise symbol greps that target the deleted fields/exports rather than broad `start`/`children` matches.
tasks.executor_notes: Deleted the five deprecated compatibility exports from `pinned-group-projection.ts`, rewrote `timeline-save-utils.test.ts` around soft-tag `clipIds`, and removed the last legacy `start`/`children` fixture inputs from `useClipEditing.test.ts`. Verified with an exact symbol/tag grep over `src/tools/video-editor` that returns no matches for the deleted exports, legacy `PinnedShotGroup` fields, or listed diagnostic log tags, then ran `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts`, which passed cleanly (5 files, 44 tests).

tasks.description: Run the full verification pass and do not stop on the first failure: create a short throwaway repro script inside the workspace that exercises the reported grouped-boundary drift and release/lost-capture symptoms, run it before and after the fixes, delete it afterward, then run the accepted validation sequence (`npx vitest run` targeted suites, grouped-flow suites, `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor`, and `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` filtered to `src/tools/video-editor/`), fixing any failures until the video-editor tree is clean.
tasks.executor_notes: Created a throwaway repro test under `src/tools/video-editor`, ran it in `REPRO_MODE=before` to reproduce both the grouped-boundary drift and lost-pointer-capture stuck-preview symptoms, then ran it in `REPRO_MODE=after` to confirm the fixed behavior and deleted it afterward. Final verification passed on the accepted sequence: targeted suites (`migrate`, `serialize`, `timeline-save-utils`, `render-bounds.validation`), grouped-flow suites (`TimelineCanvas`, `useShotGroups`, `useClipDrag`, `useClipEditing`, `useExternalDrop`, `useTimelineTrackManagement`), the full `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor` sweep (40 files, 210 tests), and the filtered TypeScript gate (`npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit --pretty false 2>&1 | rg '^src/tools/video-editor'`) with no remaining `src/tools/video-editor` diagnostics.

watch_items: Keep scope inside `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor` only; leave unrelated repo failures untouched.
Treat the two user symptoms as hard gates, not proxies: interior-boundary commits must stay stable and resize/lost-capture release must clear preview state and unblock save/poll sync.
Do not reintroduce projection-shape writes anywhere after migration; runtime commits should stay on `clipIds` plus live row actions.
For hold clips, use one rounded start/end pair so `roundedStart + roundedHold == roundedEnd`; independent rounding of `at` and `hold` is the drift source.
Lost-pointer-capture cleanup should use the same teardown path as pointer up/cancel; avoid a parallel state machine.
Keep autosave and poll-sync defer semantics intact during active interaction and ensure they re-evaluate on interaction end.
When rewriting cross-track grouped moves, explicitly update pinned-group `trackId` alongside row/meta changes so overlays and menus do not point at stale tracks.
`PinnedShotImageClipSnapshot` should only grow enough to restore images without legacy `start`/`children`; avoid unrelated metadata expansion.

Use precise cleanup verification for legacy-field deletion: grep for `PinnedShotGroup.start`, `PinnedShotGroup.children`, and deleted export names, not raw `start` or `children`, because derived shot-group APIs still legitimately use those names.
Assume `translateGroupMembers()` is the intended base for grouped move rewrites unless the code proves otherwise.
Assume filtered `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` output is the accepted video-editor TS gate; do not add a new tsconfig as part of this work.
If lost-pointer-capture fires while a grouped commit is already settling, prefer the existing end-resize path and verify it does not double-commit or leave interaction state stuck.
Net line count in shot-group-related files should go down after shim deletion; avoid compensating abstraction churn that keeps the compatibility footprint alive.
Remove only the listed diagnostic `console.log` tags; do not paper over real errors by deleting unrelated warnings or logging that still matters.

sense_checks.question: Does repeated interior-boundary commit/rematerialize cycling preserve exact adjacency between neighboring clips with no inward jump, overlap, or gap opening?
sense_checks.executor_note: The new regression serializes two adjacent grouped hold clips with a 2.3334 shared boundary through six full rematerialize cycles and asserts left end == right start on every cycle, with both clips preserving their original bounds.

sense_checks.question: If `lostpointercapture` fires mid-resize, do resize overrides clear, the resize session end, and interaction-end notification fire through the same path as pointer up/cancel?
sense_checks.executor_note: The lost-capture regression now fires `lostpointercapture` after an active resize preview and confirms the shared `endResize()` path clears the preview, ends the session, flips `interactionStateRef.resize` back to false, releases capture, decrements pending ops, calls `onActionResizeEnd`, and triggers an idle listener once.

sense_checks.question: After loading legacy projection-shaped pinned groups, are groups converted to ordered `clipIds` only, with deprecated `start`/`children` absent from the saved/emitted shape?
sense_checks.executor_note: Migration and serialization coverage now loads a projection-shaped pinned group with legacy `start` and `children`, repairs it into ordered `clipIds` only, preserves snapshot bounds, and asserts both repaired and serialized output omit `start` and `children`.

sense_checks.question: Does pinned-group sync now rebuild group membership from `Shot.images[]` generation order plus live rows, while deferring updates during active drag/resize interaction?
sense_checks.executor_note: `usePinnedGroupSync` now rebuilds image-mode group membership from `Shot.images[]` generation order plus live row actions, re-derives ordered `clipIds` from the committed rows, and reschedules itself until drag/resize interaction ends. The updated sync assertions in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts` and the live-row derivation checks in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts` cover that behavior.

sense_checks.question: Can final-video switch and image restore round-trip using only soft-tag metadata and snapshot bounds, with group `clipIds` matching the actual committed clips in both directions?
sense_checks.executor_note: The final-video switch path now snapshots each image clip’s absolute `start/end` bounds and commits `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, while the restore path rebuilds image rows from those snapshot bounds and restores the group’s image `clipIds`. Both directions are covered by the updated `useSwitchToFinalVideo` tests in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts`.

sense_checks.question: Does dropping a shot create row actions and a soft-tag pinned group in one coherent commit, with clip positions and ordered `clipIds` matching the visible result?
sense_checks.executor_note: Dropping a shot now produces one `rows` edit containing the created actions, meta updates, clip-order override, and a soft-tag pinned group whose `clipIds` match the visible committed order. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx` asserts that full result.

sense_checks.question: Do all grouped cross-track move callsites update rows, per-clip track meta, group `trackId`, and ordered `clipIds` atomically so committed state matches live preview?
sense_checks.executor_note: The remaining grouped cross-track move callsites now update rows, per-clip `track` meta, group `trackId`, and ordered `clipIds` atomically so the committed state matches the translated live group. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` covers move-to-row, create-track-and-move, and grouped arrow-key moves in both directions.

sense_checks.question: Are the five deprecated shim exports, the two legacy `PinnedShotGroup` fields, their imports/usages, and the listed diagnostic logs physically gone, with tests rewritten around soft-tag behavior?
sense_checks.executor_note: A precise grep over `src/tools/video-editor` now returns no matches for `buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `replaceGroupChildren`, `moveGroup`, `projectPinnedShotGroups`, `PinnedShotGroup.start`, `PinnedShotGroup.children`, or the listed diagnostic log tags. The remaining soft-tag coverage in `timeline-save-utils.test.ts`, `serialize.test.ts`, `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, `useClipEditing.test.ts`, and `useShotGroups.test.ts` passes on the updated expectations.

sense_checks.question: Did the throwaway repro confirm the original bugs before the fix and stop reproducing after it, and do the full video-editor tests plus filtered TypeScript gate pass cleanly?
sense_checks.executor_note: The throwaway repro test reproduced the old drift and stuck-preview states in `REPRO_MODE=before`, then confirmed adjacency preservation and lost-capture cleanup in `REPRO_MODE=after`, and was deleted afterward. The final repo state passes the targeted suites, grouped-flow suites, the full `src/tools/video-editor` Vitest run, and the filtered TypeScript gate with no remaining `src/tools/video-editor` diagnostics.

meta_commentary: Execute in the approved order because later hook rewrites depend on a stable commit path and a fully soft-tag type surface. Keep each mutation path honest: live preview and commit must use the same conceptual model, so avoid any shortcut that patches only pinned-group metadata without the corresponding rows/meta/order updates. Use `apply_patch` for edits, follow `CLAUDE.md`, and prefer tightening existing helpers over adding new layers. Treat migration as the only backward-compat entry point. When verifying deletion, search for exact symbols or type-property access patterns so legitimate derived `ShotGroup.start` / `ShotGroup.children` usage does not create false alarms. The final validation task is not a formality: if any targeted or broad suite fails, read the failure, fix it, rerun, and only finish once the reproduction script is gone, the deprecated surface is deleted, and the accepted commands are clean for `src/tools/video-editor`.

validation.completeness_notes: All 9 numbered plan steps are mapped to finalize tasks. Step 6 was intentionally split across `T6` and `T7` because external drop and grouped track management are separate production surfaces with separate tests. The final task includes the required throwaway reproduction script, targeted test commands, full `src/tools/video-editor` Vitest sweep, and filtered TypeScript verification.
